title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
The narrator discusses potential suspects near a murder scene with Colonel Beker. He identifies Mr. Lyme, an engineer living at 62 Wilbraham Crescent who travels abroad and has a wife and two sons. Beker compares Lyme's cover to a man named Pendleton, who used various wives as disguises. The narrator also mentions MacNorton, a retired Scottish professor at number 63 whose garden adjoins the murder site, and a blind former schoolteacher living alone at number 19. The narrator suggests that the proximity of these gardens would make it easy to dispose of a body. To support his investigation, the narrator presents Beker with a muddy Czech coin discovered in the back garden of number 19. Beker teases the narrator about his motives and a girl involved in the case, but the narrator remains focused on attending a trial in Crofton.

Later, Colin Lamb visits Whitehall House to see his friend, Hercule Poirot. After being greeted by a servant named George, Lamb enters room 203, where Poirot is sitting by an electric fire with tea and books. Lamb explains that he uses the alias "Lamb" to protect his father's identity. The two men discuss the aging process and gardening before transitioning to a conversation about Lamb's father, whom Poirot admires for his unique, trap-setting investigative methods. Poirot concludes the meeting by congratulating Lamb on his recent success with the Larkin case.
